About.title: About Me
About.description1: I am a backend engineer specializing in .NET and C#, focused on building APIs, modular backends, and clean architecture. I deliver reliable systems, resolve production incidents, and improve performance through incremental, well-structured changes.
About.description2: I care about maintainability, modularity, and clear design over just 'shipping' code. I have experience leading technical teams, performing code reviews, and establishing architectural standards. I automate workflows with CI/CD (GitHub Actions + Docker) and use AI-assisted tools for support, while always maintaining full responsibility for system design and validation.

Contact.title: Let's work together
Contact.description: If you're looking for someone to design and build clear, maintainable backend systems in .NET, I'd be happy to talk. You can reach me by email or through LinkedIn.

Footer.builtWith: Built with Next.js, Tailwind CSS and ☕ © {year}

hero.title: Kevin Martínez
hero.quote: Scaling isn't the problem. Uncontrolled complexity is.
hero.description: Most systems don't break at scale. They break when no one controls how complexity grows. "Architecture is the art of deciding which complexity is worth paying for."

about.title: Practical Architecture
paragraphs: Backend, Architecture, and decision making under constraints. I don't build for hypothetical scale; I build for predictable evolution.
I work on systems that are already running in production or will be. That means dealing with constraints, legacy decisions, and real business impact.
Architecture is not about drawing boxes, it is about controlling how complexity evolves over time.

items.title: Domain over Framework
items.description: Frameworks are implementation details. Business logic should not depend on them. If changing a framework breaks your system, the system was never properly designed.

items.title: Boundaries over Coupling
items.description: Shared databases are the original sin of system design. Without clear ownership and contracts, modularity is just an illusion.

items.title: Simplicity before Complexity
items.description: Microservices are not a solution, they are a cost. If a monolith is not modular, distributing it will only amplify the problem.

items.title: Re-architecting for 10x Load
items.summary: From growing monolith to modular system with controlled complexity.
items.problem: System complexity increased without clear boundaries, affecting maintainability and evolution.
items.decision: Move to modular monolith with contract-based communication.

items.title: OTP Authentication with AWS
items.summary: Decoupling authentication flow using SNS and external services.
items.problem: Tight coupling between authentication logic and core system.
items.decision: Externalized OTP via AWS SNS with retry and validation flows.

items.title: Automated Update System (WinForms)
items.summary: Forced updates with background download and integrity validation.
items.problem: Clients running outdated versions causing inconsistencies.
items.decision: Background updater with checksum validation and mandatory updates.

decisionLog.title: Decision Log
decisionLog.footerHint: /* better a monolith than a mess */

items.title: CQRS for Domain Evolution
items.body: Separated read and write models to better encapsulate business rules and allow independent evolution.

items.title: Shared DB Access
items.body: Direct access across modules created hidden coupling.

items.title: OpenTelemetry Adoption
items.body: Standardizing traces and metrics across services.

items.name: C# / .NET
items.detail: Primary environment for backend systems and architectural design

items.name: PostgreSQL / MySQL
items.detail: Relational systems with focus on data modeling and query optimization

items.name: Redis
items.detail: Cache used deliberately, not as a workaround for bad design

items.name: AWS
items.detail: Used for authentication, messaging and infrastructure support

items.title: "Microservices aren't free."
items.tag: They don't remove complexity, they relocate it.
items.body: The cost of coordination, network latency, and operational overhead is often ignored. If a team cannot manage a monolith, splitting it will not solve the problem.

items.title: "Architecture is deciding what to leave out."
items.body: Good systems are not defined by how many patterns they use, but by how many unnecessary ones they avoid.

items.title: "Clean Architecture is not enough."
items.tag: Structure without boundaries is still fragile.

items.title: "Most systems fail silently."
items.tag: Not with crashes, but with complexity.
items.body: The system keeps running, but becomes unmaintainable.

contact.title: Ready to scale without the drama?
contact.description: If you need systems that remain maintainable as they grow, we should talk.

mail.defaultMessage: Hi Kevin, reaching out from the v2 contact terminal.
